title: Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Assuming gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)
# Nipple distance = 26 cm (52 stitches apart -> Peaks at X=20 and X=72)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:25)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      # The base band (Rows 1 to 4)
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # Left Cup: Centers at stitch 20. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (39 - (Row-4)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Right Cup: Centers at stitch 72. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (53 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (91 - (Row-4)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Base Band" = "#2C3E50", "Left Cup" = "#E74C3C", "Right Cup" = "#E74C3C")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + # Keeps our stitches square for the visualization
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Underboob Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 92 sts = ~46cm)", 
    y = "Row Count (Height)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

title: Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)

# Assuming gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)

title: Underboob Top
text:
Written Pattern
Gauge: 2 HDC = 1 cm
Stitches Used: HDC (Half Double Crochet), Sl St (Slip Stitch), Ch (Chain)

Base Band

Row 1: Foundation HDC 92 (or Ch 93, HDC in 2nd ch from hook and each ch across). (92 sts)
Rows 2 - 4: Ch 1, turn. HDC in each st across. (92 sts)



Left Cup
Worked over the first 39 stitches of the Base Band.

Row 5: With the right side facing you, skip the first stitch. Attach yarn to the 2nd stitch of the Base Band. Ch 1, HDC in the same st and the next 36 sts. Leave the remaining stitches unworked. (37 sts)
Row 6: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC in the next 35 sts. Leave the last st unworked. (35 sts)
Rows 7 - 23: Repeat the process from Row 6 (turn, Sl St in the first st, Ch 1, HDC across leaving the last st unworked). Each row will decrease by 2 stitches (1 at the beginning, 1 at the end).
Final Row (Row 23): 1 HDC remains. Fasten off.



Right Cup
Worked over stitches 53 - 91 of the Base Band (leaving 13 stitches unworked between the cups).

Row 5: Skip 13 sts after the base of the Left Cup (sts 40-52). Attach yarn to stitch 54. Ch 1, HDC in the same st and the next 36 sts. Leave the last st (st 92) unworked. (37 sts)
Row 6: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC in the next 35 sts. Leave the last st unworked. (35 sts)
Rows 7 - 23: Repeat the process from Row 6, decreasing by 2 sts per row (skipping the first and last stitch) until 1 stitch remains. Fasten off.

title: Crochet Patterns
text:
Welcome to the pattern directory.

Underboob Top
Keyhole Underboob Top

title: Technical Crochet Patterns
text:
Welcome to the Technical Crochet Pattern visualization repository.
Here you will find plain-text crochet measurements and stitch descriptions translated into structured R data matrices, visualized using ggplot2.

title: Crochet Vocabulary & Abbreviations
text:
Welcome to the glossary of terms used across our technical crochet patterns.

Stitches & Techniques

HDC (Half Double Crochet)
A medium-height stitch that is taller than a single crochet but shorter than a double crochet. It creates a solid, slightly textured fabric that is perfect for underboob tops and bralettes as it balances coverage and drape.


Sl St (Slip Stitch)
A very flat, low-profile stitch. In these patterns, it is primarily used to move the yarn across the work without adding height, which creates clean, stepped decreases along the armpit edges.


Ch (Chain)
The foundation of crochet. Chains are used to create the starting row, to add height at the beginning of a new row (a turning chain), or to bridge gaps in the fabric (like the center of a keyhole cutout).


St / Sts (Stitch / Stitches)
The individual loops or “V” shapes along the top edge of your fabric.



Pattern Anatomy

Inner Edge / Cleavage Edge
The side of the cup that faces the center of the chest. In our patterns, this edge often features increases to curve the fabric inwards and create coverage.


Outer Edge / Armpit Edge
The side of the cup that faces your arm. This edge typically features steady decreases to taper the fabric from the base band up to the strap.

title: Keyhole Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width: 92 stitches
# Nipple distance: Peaks at X=20 and X=72

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:35)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      # 1. The Base Band (Solid support under the cutout)
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # 2. The Cutout Region (Rows 5 to 14)
      # Creating an arched window. The inner edges taper inwards by 2 stitches per row.
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 14 & Stitch <= (19 + 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 14 & Stitch >= (74 - 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Right)",
      
      # 3. The Sternum Bridge (Rows 15 to 18)
      # The fabric reconnects above the hole. The outer edges begin to taper in slightly.
      Row >= 15 & Row <= 18 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-14)) & Stitch <= (92 - (Row-14)) ~ "Center Bridge",
      
      # 4. The Upper Cups (Rows 19 to 35)
      # Splits back into two triangles tapering up to the neck straps (X=20 and X=72)
      Row >= 19 & Stitch >= (5 + (Row-18)) & Stitch <= (46 - (Row-18)*0.5) ~ "Upper Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 19 & Stitch >= (47 + (Row-18)*0.5) & Stitch <= (87 - (Row-18)) ~ "Upper Cup (Right)",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the updated stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Base Band" = "#2C3E50", 
    "Lower Cup (Left)" = "#E74C3C", 
    "Lower Cup (Right)" = "#E74C3C",
    "Center Bridge" = "#F1C40F",
    "Upper Cup (Left)" = "#3498DB",
    "Upper Cup (Right)" = "#3498DB"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Keyhole Underboob Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC)",
    x = "Stitch Count", 
    y = "Row Count (Bottom to Top)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom")

title: Keyhole Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width: 92 stitches
# Nipple distance: Peaks at X=20 and X=72

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:35)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      # 1. The Base Band (Solid support under the cutout)
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # 2. The Cutout Region (Rows 5 to 14)
      # Creating an arched window. The inner edges taper inwards by 2 stitches per row.
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 14 & Stitch <= (19 + 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 5 & Row <= 14 & Stitch >= (74 - 2*(Row-5)) ~ "Lower Cup (Right)",
      
      # 3. The Sternum Bridge (Rows 15 to 18)
      # The fabric reconnects above the hole. The outer edges begin to taper in slightly.
      Row >= 15 & Row <= 18 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-14)) & Stitch <= (92 - (Row-14)) ~ "Center Bridge",
      
      # 4. The Upper Cups (Rows 19 to 35)
      # Splits back into two triangles tapering up to the neck straps (X=20 and X=72)
      Row >= 19 & Stitch >= (5 + (Row-18)) & Stitch <= (46 - (Row-18)*0.5) ~ "Upper Cup (Left)",
      Row >= 19 & Stitch >= (47 + (Row-18)*0.5) & Stitch <= (87 - (Row-18)) ~ "Upper Cup (Right)",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the updated stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Base Band" = "#2C3E50", 
    "Lower Cup (Left)" = "#E74C3C", 
    "Lower Cup (Right)" = "#E74C3C",
    "Center Bridge" = "#F1C40F",
    "Upper Cup (Left)" = "#3498DB",
    "Upper Cup (Right)" = "#3498DB"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Keyhole Underboob Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC)",
    x = "Stitch Count", 
    y = "Row Count (Bottom to Top)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom")

title: Keyhole Underboob Top
text:
Written Pattern
Gauge: 2 HDC = 1 cm
Stitches Used: HDC (Half Double Crochet), Sl St (Slip Stitch), Ch (Chain)

1. Base Band

Rows 1 - 4: Foundation HDC 92. (Or Ch 93, HDC in 2nd ch from hook and each ch across). For rows 2-4: Ch 1, turn, HDC across. (92 sts)



2. The Cutout Region (Sides of the Window)
You will work the left and right sides separately, leaving an 18-stitch gap in the middle.
Left Side: - Row 5: With right side facing, Ch 1. HDC in the first 17 sts. Work 3 HDC in the next st (st 18). Leave the rest unworked. (19 sts) - Rows 6 - 14: Ch 1, turn. On the inner edge (the cleavage side), work 3 HDC into the first or last stitch to increase by 2 sts per row. HDC in each remaining st. By Row 14, you will have grown to 37 sts. Fasten off after Row 14.
Right Side: - Row 5: Skip the next 54 sts of the Base Band (this wide gap forms the bottom of the arched keyhole window). Attach yarn to the 74th stitch. Work 3 HDC in the same st. HDC in the remaining 18 sts. (19 sts) - Rows 6 - 14: Ch 1, turn. Just like the Left Side, work 3 HDC into the stitch on the inner cleavage edge to increase by 2 sts per row. HDC in each remaining st. Do not fasten off; you will continue from here with 37 sts.


3. The Sternum Bridge (Closing the Window)

Row 15: (Continuing from the right side). Ch 1, turn. Skip the first st. HDC in the next 36 sts of the Right Side. Ch 18 over the keyhole gap. HDC across the 37 sts of the Left Side until 1 st remains. Leave the last st unworked. (90 sts counting the chains)
Rows 16 - 18: Ch 1, turn. Skip the first st. HDC across (working into the HDCs and the chains from the previous row) until 1 st remains. Leave the last st unworked. (Each row decreases by 2 stitches. By Row 18, you will have 84 sts).



4. The Upper Cups (Triangles)
You will split the work again to form the two upper triangles.
Left Upper Cup: - Row 19: With right side facing, Ch 1. Skip the first st. HDC in the next 40 sts. Leave the rest unworked. (40 sts) - Rows 20 - 35: Ch 1, turn. Decrease 1 st at the armpit edge (outer edge), and alternate decreasing 0 or 1 st at the cleavage edge (inner edge) to follow the taper of the grid (a ratio of roughly 1:0.5 decreases). Continue until only a few stitches remain for the strap. Fasten off.
Right Upper Cup: - Row 19: Attach yarn to the 48th stitch (leaving a small gap in the center). Ch 1, HDC in the next 40 sts, leaving the last st unworked. (40 sts) - Rows 20 - 35: Ch 1, turn. Decrease 1 st at the armpit edge, and alternate decreasing 0 or 1 st at the cleavage edge. Continue until only a few stitches remain. Fasten off.
